verdict.answer_first: A states the actionable answer under a clear heading, 'What to do depends on intent', while B buries its suggestion after incorrect information and a follow-up question, 'If you tell me which of those two you're after, I can suggest the exact replacement.'
verdict.decisiveness: A commits to a specific, improved regex for the most common use case: 'tighten it slightly so it can't match whitespace or multiple @'. B defers the decision: 'If you tell me which... I can suggest the exact replacement.'
verdict.fabrication: B invents a bug: 'In JavaScript $ matches at the end of the string or just before a final \n... so "foo@bar\n" passes.' This is false for JavaScript's default regex engine behavior.
verdict.actionability: A is immediately actionable. It correctly diagnoses the problem, debunks a common red herring, and provides a copy-pasteable, improved regex. B's factual error and indecisiveness make it untrustworthy and slower to act on.
